response.justification: The candidate brings tangential geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map GIS work, and has experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby, but fundamentally misses the core requirements of this role. C++ experience is limited to a single 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; there is no Rust experience. The candidate's primary and recent expertise is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack and web development, which is a fundamentally different domain from the systems-level, C++-driven geospatial routing pipeline this role demands. No routing, navigation, or tiling pipeline work is present in the resume.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective platform and Green Map GIS application development
Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed micro-services handling millions of requests per second (Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied consistently across roles
concerns: No meaningful C++ or Rust experience; last C++ work was a 6-month role in 2014–2015 with no follow-up
No routing, navigation, or tiling pipeline experience; GIS work targets conservation data collection, not routing optimization
Entire recent career (2018–present) is TypeScript/Node.js/web-stack — misaligned with the systems programming core of this role

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline work
